11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
91		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
92		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
93		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
94			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
95		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
96		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
97		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99	
100		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
101		// and "ddx work metrics" as subcommands.
102		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
103		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
104		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
105		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
106	
107		return cmd
108	}
109	
110	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
111	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
112	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
113	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
114	// by this layer.
115	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
116		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
117			return err
118		}
119	
120		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
121		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
122	
123		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
124		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
125		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
126		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
127		if !asJSON {
128			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
129			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
130				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
131			})
132		}
133	
134		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
135	}

3	import (
4		"fmt"

6		"strings"

8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/attemptmetrics"

13	// beadSpecID extracts the spec-id from a bead's Extra map, returning "" when
14	// absent or not a string.
15	func beadSpecID(b bead.Bead) string {
16		raw, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string)
17		return strings.TrimSpace(raw)
18	}
19	
20	// newWorkMetricsCommand returns the "ddx work metrics" parent command with
21	// backfill as its only subcommand.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkMetricsCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "metrics",
25			Short: "Metrics over attempt evidence",
26			Long:  "Manage .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl — the cold-tier per-attempt metrics store.",
27			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
28				_ = cmd.Help()
29			},
30		}
31		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand())
32		return cmd
33	}
34	
35	// newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand returns "ddx work metrics backfill". It reads
36	// all beads' events.jsonl attachments and emits one row per attempt found,
37	// deduplicating against any rows already in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
38	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand() *cobra.Command {
39		cmd := &cobra.Command{
40			Use:   "backfill",
41			Short: "Backfill .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl from existing bead events",
42			Long: `Scan all beads' events and produce per-attempt rows in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
43	
44	Each kind:cost event in a bead's event stream corresponds to one agent execution
45	attempt. backfill pairs each cost event with the immediately following
46	kind:execute-bead event (the finalization outcome) to produce a complete row.
47	
48	The command is idempotent: rows already present in attempts.jsonl (identified by
49	attempt_id) are skipped. Re-running backfill after a partial run or after new
50	beads are closed is safe.
51	
52	Note: backfill reads .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl and the inline
53	events in beads.jsonl. It does NOT read .ddx/executions/ directories.`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
56				projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
57				projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
58	
59				store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
60				allBeads, err := store.ReadAll()
61				if err != nil {
62					return fmt.Errorf("read beads: %w", err)
63				}
64	
65				inputs := make([]attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents, 0, len(allBeads))
66				for _, b := range allBeads {
67					events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
68					if err != nil {
69						// Best-effort: skip beads whose events can't be read.
70						if f.WorkingDir != "" {
71							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
72								"warning: skipping %s events: %v\n", b.ID, err)
73						}
74						continue
75					}
76					inputs = append(inputs, attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents{
77						BeadID: b.ID,
78						SpecID: beadSpecID(b),
79						Events: events,
80					})
81				}
82	
83				added, err := attemptmetrics.BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot, inputs)
84				if err != nil {
85					return fmt.Errorf("backfill: %w", err)
86				}
87				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "backfill complete: %d new rows added\n", added)
88				return nil
89			},
90		}
91		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root)")
92		return cmd
93	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"text/tabwriter"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
13	)
14	
15	// newWorkPlanCommand creates the "ddx work plan" subcommand: a read-only
16	// dry-run preview of the queue showing the exact ordering "ddx work" would
17	// pick, without claiming or executing any bead.
18	//
19	// Relationship to siblings:
20	//   - "ddx work"       — executes the drain (claims and runs beads)
21	//   - "ddx work plan"  — previews what the drain would do (read-only)
22	//   - "ddx bead ready" — lists dependency-ready beads; does NOT apply the
23	//     picker's label-filter or cooldown/superseded eligibility filters
24	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkPlanCommand() *cobra.Command {
25		cmd := &cobra.Command{
26			Use:   "plan",
27			Short: "Preview what 'ddx work' would pick (dry-run)",
28			Long: `plan shows the execution queue in picker order — the same order
29	'ddx work' would claim beads — without claiming or executing anything.
30	
31	Unlike 'ddx bead ready', plan applies the picker's filters:
32	  - Execution eligibility (cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible flag)
33	  - Optional label-filter intersection (--label-filter)
34	  - Optional capabilities constraint (--capabilities; currently a no-op)
35	
36	The per-Run attempted map is intentionally excluded: it is non-deterministic
37	across runs and is not part of the stable picker decision surface.
38	
39	Columns:
40	  POS      — 1-based pick order
41	  ID       — bead ID
42	  TITLE    — bead title
43	  PRI      — priority (0 = highest)
44	  RANK     — queue-rank within the priority bucket, when present
45	  UPDATED  — last updated timestamp (RFC3339)
46	  STATUS   — bead status
47	  DECISION — "next claim", "eligible (rank N)", or "skipped: <reason>"
48	
49	Use --json for machine-readable output suitable for piping to jq:
50	  ddx work plan --json | jq '.[].id'
51	`,
52			Example: `  # Show top 10 beads in picker order (default)
53	  ddx work plan
54	
55	  # Show the full queue
56	  ddx work plan --limit=0
57	
58	  # Filter identically to a label-constrained worker
59	  ddx work plan --label-filter=phase:2,area:agent
60	
61	  # Machine-readable output
62	  ddx work plan --json | jq '.[].id'`,
63			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
64			RunE: f.runWorkPlan,
65		}
66	
67		cmd.Flags().String("label-filter", "", "Filter by label (same intersection logic as 'ddx work --label-filter')")
68		cmd.Flags().String("capabilities", "", "Capabilities constraint (reserved; currently no-op pass-through)")
69		cmd.Flags().Int("limit", 10, "Maximum number of entries to show; 0 = full queue")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON array (suitable for piping to jq)")
71	
72		return cmd
73	}
74	
75	// runWorkPlan is the RunE for "ddx work plan".
76	func (f *CommandFactory) runWorkPlan(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
77		labelFilter, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("label-filter")
78		capabilities, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("capabilities")
79		limit, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("limit")
80		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
81	
82		// Resolve the project root (same logic work.go uses).
83		projectRoot := f.WorkingDir
84	
85		// Open the bead store directly — same path ReadyExecution uses.
86		ddxDir := projectRoot + "/.ddx"
87		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
88	
89		filters := agent.PickerFilters{
90			LabelFilter:  labelFilter,
91			Capabilities: capabilities,
92		}
93	
94		entries, err := agent.PreviewQueue(store, filters, limit)
95		if err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("work plan: %w", err)
97		}
98		var breakdown *bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown
99		if diag, ok := any(store).(interface {
100			ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
101		}); ok {
102			if b, derr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); derr == nil {
103				breakdown = &b
104			}
105		}
106	
107		if asJSON {
108			return printWorkPlanJSON(cmd, entries)
109		}
110		return printWorkPlanText(cmd, entries, breakdown, limit)
111	}
112	
113	func printWorkPlanJSON(cmd *cobra.Command, entries []agent.QueueEntry) error {
114		enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
115		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
116		return enc.Encode(entries)
117	}
118	
119	func printWorkPlanText(cmd *cobra.Command, entries []agent.QueueEntry, breakdown *bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, limit int) error {
120		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
121		if len(entries) == 0 {
122			fmt.Fprintln(out, "No execution-eligible beads in the queue.")
123		}
124	
125		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(out, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
126		fmt.Fprintln(w, "POS\tID\tTITLE\tPRI\tRANK\tUPDATED\tSTATUS\tDECISION\tWHY")
127		fmt.Fprintln(w, "---\t--\t-----\t---\t----\t-------\t------\t--------\t---")
128		for _, e := range entries {
129			updated := e.UpdatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
130			if e.UpdatedAt.IsZero() {
131				updated = "-"
132			}
133			rank := "-"
134			if e.QueueRank != nil {
135				rank = fmt.Sprintf("%d", *e.QueueRank)
136			}
137			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n",
138				e.Position, e.BeadID, e.Title, e.Priority, rank, updated, e.Status, e.FilterDecision, e.Why)
139		}
140		_ = w.Flush()
141	
142		if breakdown != nil {
143			if len(breakdown.Epics) > 0 {
144				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  skipped %d ready epic(s) with open children (epics are structural containers; decompose into tasks): %s\n",
145					len(breakdown.Epics), strings.Join(breakdown.Epics, ", "))
146			}
147			if len(breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
148				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
149					strings.Join(breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
150			}
151		}
152	
153		if limit > 0 {
154			fmt.Fprintf(out, "\n(showing up to %d entries; use --limit=0 for full queue)\n", limit)
155		}
156		return nil
157	}

1	// Package attemptmetrics implements the cold-tier metrics layer: one row per
2	// bead-attempt written to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Rows are appended with
3	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer access is safe. The schema_version field
4	// allows future migrations without losing historical rows.
5	package attemptmetrics
6	
7	import (
8		"bufio"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15	)
16	
17	const (
18		SchemaVersion   = 1
19		attemptsRelPath = ".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl"
20	)
21	
22	// AttemptRow is one row in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
23	type AttemptRow struct {
24		SchemaVersion int    `json:"schema_version"`
25		AttemptID     string `json:"attempt_id"`
26		BeadID        string `json:"bead_id"`
27		SessionID     string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
28		TSStart       string `json:"ts_start,omitempty"` // RFC3339
29		TSEnd         string `json:"ts_end,omitempty"`   // RFC3339
30	
31		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
32		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
33		Profile  string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
34		Provider string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
35	
36		// PromptTemplateID and PromptTemplateHash identify the prompt variant used.
37		PromptTemplateID   string `json:"prompt_template_id,omitempty"`
38		PromptTemplateHash string `json:"prompt_template_hash,omitempty"` // hex sha256 of rendered prompt
39	
40		SpecID string `json:"spec_id,omitempty"`
41	
42		Outcome  string  `json:"outcome"`
43		ExitCode int     `json:"exit_code"`
44		CostUSD  float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
45	
46		DurationMS   int `json:"duration_ms"`
47		InputTokens  int `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
48		OutputTokens int `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
49		TotalTokens  int `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
50	
51		ReviewVerdict                string         `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
52		ReviewFindingCountBySeverity map[string]int `json:"review_finding_count_by_severity,omitempty"`
53	
54		LadderMinPowerInitial int `json:"ladder_min_power_initial,omitempty"`
55		LadderMinPowerFinal   int `json:"ladder_min_power_final,omitempty"`
56		LadderStepsTaken      int `json:"ladder_steps_taken,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// BeadAttemptEvents is the raw input for BackfillFromEvents: a bead ID, its
60	// optional spec-id, and its full event stream.
61	type BeadAttemptEvents struct {
62		BeadID string
63		SpecID string // from bead Extra["spec-id"], optional
64		Events []bead.BeadEvent
65	}
66	
67	// costEventBody matches the JSON shape written by appendBeadCostEvidence.
68	type costEventBody struct {
69		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
70		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
71		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
72		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
73		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens"`
74		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens"`
75		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
76		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
77		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
78		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
79	}
80	
81	// AttemptsPath returns the absolute path to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
82	// under projectRoot.
83	func AttemptsPath(projectRoot string) string {
84		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, attemptsRelPath)
85	}
86	
87	// AppendRow appends row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl under projectRoot using
88	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
89	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
90	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
91		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
92		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
100		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
101		if err != nil {
102			return err
103		}

171	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
172	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
173		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
174		if err != nil {
175			return 0, err
176		}

179		for _, b := range beads {

183					continue
184				}

195	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
196	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
197	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
198	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
199		var rows []AttemptRow
200		for i, ev := range b.Events {
201			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
202				continue
203			}
204			var cost costEventBody
205			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
206				continue
207			}
208	
209			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
210			outcome := ""
211			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
212				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
213					outcome = next.Summary
214					break
215				}
216			}
217	
218			tsEnd := ""
219			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
220				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
221			}
222			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
223			tsStart := ""
224			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
225				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
226				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
227			}
228	
229			total := cost.TotalTokens
230			if total == 0 {
231				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
232			}
233	
234			row := AttemptRow{
235				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
236				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
237				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
238				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
239				TSStart:       tsStart,
240				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
241				Model:         cost.Model,
242				Harness:       cost.Harness,
243				Provider:      cost.Provider,
244				Outcome:       outcome,
245				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
246				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
247				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
248				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
249				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
250				TotalTokens:   total,
251			}
252			rows = append(rows, row)
253		}
254		return rows
255	}

257	// Rfc3339 formats a time.Time as RFC3339 UTC; returns "" for the zero value.
258	func Rfc3339(t time.Time) string {
259		if t.IsZero() {
260			return ""
261		}
262		return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)

93		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
94			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
95		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
96		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
97		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99	
100		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
101		// and "ddx work metrics" as subcommands.
102		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
103		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
104		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
105		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
106	
107		return cmd
108	}
109	
110	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
111	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
112	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
113	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
114	// by this layer.
115	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
116		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
117			return err
118		}
119	
120		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
121		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
122	
123		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
124		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
125		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
126		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
127		if !asJSON {
128			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
129			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
130				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
131			})
132		}
133	
134		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
135	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/attemptmetrics"
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// beadSpecID extracts the spec-id from a bead's Extra map, returning "" when
14	// absent or not a string.
15	func beadSpecID(b bead.Bead) string {
16		raw, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string)
17		return strings.TrimSpace(raw)
18	}
19	
20	// newWorkMetricsCommand returns the "ddx work metrics" parent command with
21	// backfill as its only subcommand.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkMetricsCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "metrics",
25			Short: "Metrics over attempt evidence",
26			Long:  "Manage .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl — the cold-tier per-attempt metrics store.",
27			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
28				_ = cmd.Help()
29			},
30		}
31		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand())
32		return cmd
33	}
34	
35	// newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand returns "ddx work metrics backfill". It reads
36	// all beads' events.jsonl attachments and emits one row per attempt found,
37	// deduplicating against any rows already in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
38	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand() *cobra.Command {
39		cmd := &cobra.Command{
40			Use:   "backfill",
41			Short: "Backfill .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl from existing bead events",
42			Long: `Scan all beads' events and produce per-attempt rows in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
43	
44	Each kind:cost event in a bead's event stream corresponds to one agent execution
45	attempt. backfill pairs each cost event with the immediately following
46	kind:execute-bead event (the finalization outcome) to produce a complete row.
47	
48	The command is idempotent: rows already present in attempts.jsonl (identified by
49	attempt_id) are skipped. Re-running backfill after a partial run or after new
50	beads are closed is safe.
51	
52	Note: backfill reads .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl and the inline
53	events in beads.jsonl. It does NOT read .ddx/executions/ directories.`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
56				projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
57				projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
58	
59				store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
60				allBeads, err := store.ReadAll()
61				if err != nil {
62					return fmt.Errorf("read beads: %w", err)
63				}
64	
65				inputs := make([]attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents, 0, len(allBeads))
66				for _, b := range allBeads {
67					events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
68					if err != nil {
69						// Best-effort: skip beads whose events can't be read.
70						if f.WorkingDir != "" {
71							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
72								"warning: skipping %s events: %v\n", b.ID, err)
73						}
74						continue
75					}
76					inputs = append(inputs, attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents{
77						BeadID: b.ID,
78						SpecID: beadSpecID(b),
79						Events: events,
80					})
81				}
82	
83				added, err := attemptmetrics.BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot, inputs)
84				if err != nil {
85					return fmt.Errorf("backfill: %w", err)
86				}
87				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "backfill complete: %d new rows added\n", added)
88				return nil
89			},
90		}
91		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root)")
92		return cmd
93	}

1	// Package attemptmetrics implements the cold-tier metrics layer: one row per
2	// bead-attempt written to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Rows are appended with
3	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer access is safe. The schema_version field
4	// allows future migrations without losing historical rows.
5	package attemptmetrics
6	
7	import (
8		"bufio"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15	)
16	
17	const (
18		SchemaVersion   = 1
19		attemptsRelPath = ".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl"
20	)
21	
22	// AttemptRow is one row in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
23	type AttemptRow struct {
24		SchemaVersion int    `json:"schema_version"`
25		AttemptID     string `json:"attempt_id"`
26		BeadID        string `json:"bead_id"`
27		SessionID     string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
28		TSStart       string `json:"ts_start,omitempty"` // RFC3339
29		TSEnd         string `json:"ts_end,omitempty"`   // RFC3339
30	
31		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
32		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
33		Profile  string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
34		Provider string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
35	
36		// PromptTemplateID and PromptTemplateHash identify the prompt variant used.
37		PromptTemplateID   string `json:"prompt_template_id,omitempty"`
38		PromptTemplateHash string `json:"prompt_template_hash,omitempty"` // hex sha256 of rendered prompt
39	
40		SpecID string `json:"spec_id,omitempty"`
41	
42		Outcome  string  `json:"outcome"`
43		ExitCode int     `json:"exit_code"`
44		CostUSD  float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
45	
46		DurationMS   int `json:"duration_ms"`
47		InputTokens  int `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
48		OutputTokens int `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
49		TotalTokens  int `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
50	
51		ReviewVerdict                string         `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
52		ReviewFindingCountBySeverity map[string]int `json:"review_finding_count_by_severity,omitempty"`
53	
54		LadderMinPowerInitial int `json:"ladder_min_power_initial,omitempty"`
55		LadderMinPowerFinal   int `json:"ladder_min_power_final,omitempty"`
56		LadderStepsTaken      int `json:"ladder_steps_taken,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// BeadAttemptEvents is the raw input for BackfillFromEvents: a bead ID, its
60	// optional spec-id, and its full event stream.
61	type BeadAttemptEvents struct {
62		BeadID string
63		SpecID string // from bead Extra["spec-id"], optional
64		Events []bead.BeadEvent
65	}
66	
67	// costEventBody matches the JSON shape written by appendBeadCostEvidence.
68	type costEventBody struct {
69		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
70		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
71		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
72		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
73		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens"`
74		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens"`
75		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
76		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
77		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
78		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
79	}
80	
81	// AttemptsPath returns the absolute path to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
82	// under projectRoot.
83	func AttemptsPath(projectRoot string) string {
84		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, attemptsRelPath)
85	}
86	
87	// AppendRow appends row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl under projectRoot using
88	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
89	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
90	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
91		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
92		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
100		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
101		if err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104		data = append(data, '\n')
105		_, err = f.Write(data)
106		return err
107	}
108	
109	// LoadAttemptIDs reads .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl and returns the set of
110	// attempt_ids already present. Used to deduplicate BackfillFromEvents runs.
111	// Returns an empty map when the file does not exist.
112	func LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot string) (map[string]struct{}, error) {
113		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
114		f, err := os.Open(path)
115		if err != nil {
116			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
117				return map[string]struct{}{}, nil
118			}
119			return nil, err
120		}
121		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
122	
123		seen := map[string]struct{}{}
124		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
125		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024) // 1 MiB lines max
126		for sc.Scan() {
127			var row struct {
128				AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id"`
129			}
130			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil || row.AttemptID == "" {
131				continue
132			}
133			seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
134		}
135		return seen, sc.Err()
136	}
137	
138	// LoadRows reads all rows from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Malformed lines
139	// are skipped. Returns an empty slice when the file does not exist.
140	func LoadRows(projectRoot string) ([]AttemptRow, error) {
141		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
142		f, err := os.Open(path)
143		if err != nil {
144			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
145				return []AttemptRow{}, nil
146			}
147			return nil, err
148		}
149		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
150	
151		var rows []AttemptRow
152		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
153		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024)
154		for sc.Scan() {
155			var row AttemptRow
156			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil {
157				continue
158			}
159			rows = append(rows, row)
160		}
161		return rows, sc.Err()
162	}
163	
164	// BackfillFromEvents processes beads' event streams, appending one AttemptRow
165	// per attempt found (identified by kind:cost events). Deduplicates by
166	// attempt_id: rows already in attempts.jsonl are skipped. Returns the count of
167	// new rows added.
168	//
169	// The matching algorithm: for each kind:cost event, scan forward to find the
170	// next kind:execute-bead event (which carries the finalization outcome). This
171	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
172	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
173		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
174		if err != nil {
175			return 0, err
176		}
177	
178		var added int
179		for _, b := range beads {
180			rows := extractRowsFromEvents(b)
181			for _, row := range rows {
182				if _, exists := seen[row.AttemptID]; exists {
183					continue
184				}
185				if err := AppendRow(projectRoot, row); err != nil {
186					return added, err
187				}
188				seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
189				added++
190			}
191		}
192		return added, nil
193	}
194	
195	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
196	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
197	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
198	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
199		var rows []AttemptRow
200		for i, ev := range b.Events {
201			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
202				continue
203			}
204			var cost costEventBody
205			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
206				continue
207			}
208	
209			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
210			outcome := ""
211			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
212				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
213					outcome = next.Summary
214					break
215				}
216			}
217	
218			tsEnd := ""
219			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
220				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
221			}
222			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
223			tsStart := ""
224			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
225				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
226				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
227			}
228	
229			total := cost.TotalTokens
230			if total == 0 {
231				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
232			}
233	
234			row := AttemptRow{
235				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
236				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
237				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
238				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
239				TSStart:       tsStart,
240				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
241				Model:         cost.Model,
242				Harness:       cost.Harness,
243				Provider:      cost.Provider,
244				Outcome:       outcome,
245				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
246				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
247				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
248				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
249				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
250				TotalTokens:   total,
251			}
252			rows = append(rows, row)
253		}
254		return rows
255	}
256	
257	// Rfc3339 formats a time.Time as RFC3339 UTC; returns "" for the zero value.
258	func Rfc3339(t time.Time) string {
259		if t.IsZero() {
260			return ""
261		}
262		return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
263	}
